% Generated metadata -- regenerated every run.
% NOTE (achemso-specific, see preamble.tex.j2 + manifest.yaml's ARCHITECTURE
% FINDING): this file is \input TWICE -- once early, from preamble.tex.j2,
% while still genuinely in the LaTeX preamble (satisfying achemso's
% \@onlypreamble \title/\author/\affiliation/\email/\keywords), and once for
% real, from main.tex, after \begin{document}. \ifdefined tells the two
% reads apart: the first defines \LtxAchemsoMetaLoaded and emits the
% preamble-only metadata macros; the second (flag already defined) emits only
% \begin{abstract}...\end{abstract} + \maketitle, which -- unlike the
% metadata setters -- MUST run in the body (\begin{abstract} itself errors
% "Missing \begin{document}" if attempted early; verified empirically).
\ifdefined\LtxAchemsoMetaLoaded
\begin{abstract}
We report anomalous transport signatures in a two-dimensional electron gas and analyze their temperature dependence.
\end{abstract}
\maketitle
\else
\def\LtxAchemsoMetaLoaded{1}%
\title{Anomalous Transport in a Two-Dimensional Electron Gas}
\author{Alice Anderson}
\email{alice.anderson@university-a.edu}
\affiliation{Department of Physics, University A, City A, Country A}
\affiliation{National High Field Laboratory, City B, Country B}
\author{Bob Baker}
\affiliation{National High Field Laboratory, City B, Country B}
\keywords{transport, two-dimensional electron gas}
\fi
